key_insight: The paper learns fast approximate solution maps for multiparametric generalized Nash equilibrium problems by training a neural network with a Nikaido-Isoda gap-based loss instead of requiring full equilibrium labels.
engineering_relevance: This enables near-instant online equilibrium prediction for coupled multi-agent systems, with potential use in control, energy, and networked optimization settings where classical solvers are too slow.
method: Use the Nikaido-Isoda gap as the training objective so only best-response information is needed.
Replace bilevel best-response optimization with a learned value-function surrogate to obtain a single-level learning problem.
Extend the approach to standard multiparametric programming as a special case and derive continuity conditions via strong variational stability.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments across multiple problem classes and claims speedups of several orders of magnitude over online solvers.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the value-function surrogate and on assumptions for continuous parametric equilibrium selection, so difficult instances may be less reliable.
paper_title: Learning Approximate Solutions to Multiparametric Generalized Nash Equilibrium Problems
